content:
similar note for minor correction which also needs to be fixed:
hameln exalt wording should be changed to "non-temporary" instead of specifying exhaust, now that saya allows for destroy-tagged cards to be created
(assuming that hameln can't rondino saya cards, which I presume is the case, but needs to be tested after release first)
alternatively, the term could be non-consumable or permanent cards? idk if we have an existing term for non-exhaust non-destroy (and probably non-fleeting)

Noted. Will need to playtest Saya to determine what my suggested fixes would be, but my inclination at this point is to lean further on defining and naming absolute properties and only using those names when it's code-correct to do so. "Temporary" is used for a lot of things right now, and it sounds like it's about to make a new problem happen
yeah fundamentally speaking hameln exalt is very clearly designed to work on base cards but that's just not like, an established term yet so there's no easy marker for it
I like the idea of reinforcing the designation "Persistent" so that we can refer to any card that does not stay in the deck after battle ends as "non-Persistent"
whether that be fleeting, exhaust, or otherwise like Hysteria
specifying non-derivative non-temporary command card is honestly kind of silly if we could just say specify 1 base card instead
and "Destroy" cards would describe themselves as Persistent
